purpose: Permanent channel-wide visual canon for long-running dark fantasy lore production.

must_pass: clearly communicates the intended script beat
contains the required key story elements
matches the dark fantasy lore tone
has strong composition and focal clarity
hard_fail: breaks the Ember & Stone visual style
contains readable text, fake letters, logos, signatures, stamps, or watermarks
has distracting anatomy or composition problems
feels cartoonish, goofy, bright, sci-fi, anime, or superheroic
acceptable_flexibility: paper on floor instead of directly on floor
symbolic interpretation instead of exact staging
aftermath instead of direct action when it supports the beat
background simplified to improve focal clarity

generation_instruction: This image must match the permanent visual identity of the Ember & Stone channel. Ember & Stone is a dark fantasy lore channel with a consistent, production-grade visual style. Every image must feel like it belongs to the same branded universe across the entire channel, not just this one video. Use dark fantasy lore illustration, cinematic moody atmosphere, mythic ancient tone, muted charcoal ash stone fog-blue parchment palette, restrained amber candlelight accents, soft atmospheric lighting, and strong clear composition. Environments should feel ancient, worn, forgotten, and story-rich. Characters should fit a grounded dark fantasy world. Prioritize clarity, beauty, narrative relevance, and visual cohesion. Do not drift into modern objects, modern fashion, sci-fi, bright high-saturation fantasy, playful cartoon style, random style switching, inconsistent rendering quality, generic filler imagery, readable text, letters, logos, signatures, stamps, watermarks, official D&D art style, or copyrighted character lookalikes. The image must look amazing, cinematic, emotionally resonant, and clearly belong to the Ember & Stone channel.
long_running_rule: This style bible is permanent until intentionally revised. The channel is expected to run indefinitely, so visual decisions must support repeatable production, long-term consistency, future automation, and human review gates.
